The qualities of Kṛṣṇa are superior to those of Rāmacandra
The same dhīrodātta qualities seen in Rāmacandra are also present in Kṛṣṇa
They are identical in manifestation regardless of the devotee's mood
questions.correct_answer: The same dhīrodātta qualities seen in Rāmacandra are also present in Kṛṣṇa
questions.explanation: The text states that these same qualities [dhīrodātta] are also seen in Kṛṣṇa, showing their presence in both manifestations.

2.1.230.verse_text: He is called dhīra-lalita who is clever, endowed with fresh youth (end of kaiśora period), skilful at joking and free of worries. He is controlled by His dear devotees.

questions.question: Which specific term is used to describe a hero who is characterized by being clever, youthful, humorous, and carefree?
questions.explanation: A dhīra-lalita hero is defined as one who is clever, youthful, joking, and free of anxieties.

questions.question: What is the specific developmental stage of youth attributed to the dhīra-lalita hero?
questions.explanation: The dhīra-lalita hero is described as being endowed with fresh youth at the end of the kaiśora period.

questions.question: Which personality trait regarding emotional state is a defining mark of the dhīra-lalita hero?
questions.explanation: Being free of worries is one of the four essential characteristics of the dhīra-lalita hero.

questions.question: In terms of social interaction, what skill is attributed to the dhīra-lalita hero?
questions.explanation: A dhīra-lalita hero is explicitly noted for being skilful at joking with others.

questions.question: What is the unique relationship the dhīra-lalita hero maintains with His dear devotees?
questions.explanation: The dhīra-lalita hero is distinguished by the fact that He is controlled by the love of His dear devotees.

2.1.231.verse_text:
An example:

Kṛṣṇa made Rādhā lower her eyes in shame by boldly describing their pastimes of the previous night in front of her friends. Taking that opportunity, He displayed His skill by expertly drawing frolicking makaris on her breasts. In this way Kṛṣṇa sported in the groves and fulfilled His youthful years.

questions.question: What specific action did Kṛṣṇa perform to cause Rādhā to lower her eyes in shame?
options: He boldly described their pastimes of the previous night in front of her friends
He hid her garments during her bath
He teased her about her service to the gopīs
questions.correct_answer: He boldly described their pastimes of the previous night in front of her friends
questions.explanation: Kṛṣṇa caused Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī to lower her eyes out of modesty by recounting the intimate details of their previous night's pastimes before her friends.

questions.question: Which artistic skill did Kṛṣṇa exhibit upon Rādhā's person after she lowered her eyes in shame?
options: He decorated her hair with forest flowers
He applied sandalwood paste designs on her forehead
He expertly drew frolicking makaris on her breasts
He painted intricate patterns on her hands with henna
questions.correct_answer: He expertly drew frolicking makaris on her breasts
questions.explanation: Taking advantage of the moment when Rādhā lowered her eyes in shame, Kṛṣṇa skillfully drew makari designs on her breasts.

questions.question: Where did the described intimate sporting pastimes between Kṛṣṇa and Rādhā take place?
options: On the banks of the Yamunā
Within the mountain caves of Govardhana
On the stage of the Rāsa dance
questions.explanation: The text explicitly mentions that Kṛṣṇa sported in the groves while engaging in these youthful pastimes.

questions.question: What primary purpose did Kṛṣṇa's sporting in the groves serve according to the description?
options: He was teaching His friends about art
He was fulfilling His youthful years
He was practicing for the dance of the gopīs
He was waiting for the arrival of the cowherd boys
questions.correct_answer: He was fulfilling His youthful years
questions.explanation: The description concludes by stating that through these activities in the groves, Kṛṣṇa fulfilled His youthful years.

2.1.232.verse_text: The qualities of the dhīra-lalita are clearly revealed in Kṛṣṇa. But the dramatic scholars give the example of Cupid.

questions.question: Which specific personality is identified as the supreme example of the dhīra-lalita disposition according to the nectar of devotion?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the qualities of the dhīra-lalita are clearly revealed in Kṛṣṇa.

questions.question: Who is cited by dramatic scholars as a general example of the dhīra-lalita personality type?
questions.explanation: The text notes that although Kṛṣṇa perfectly embodies the dhīra-lalita nature, dramatic scholars typically use Cupid as the standard example for this category.

2.1.233.verse_text: The learned say that He who is peaceful, tolerates suffering, uses discrimination, and possesses qualities like modesty is called dhīra-śānta.

questions.question: Which of the following characteristics is identified as a primary quality of a dhīra-śānta personality?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly defines the dhīra-śānta as one who is peaceful.

questions.question: Beyond being peaceful and using discrimination, what is a specific hallmark of the dhīra-śānta nature mentioned in the text?
questions.explanation: The text defines a dhīra-śānta as one who tolerates suffering along with other saintly traits.

questions.question: What mental faculty does the dhīra-śānta employ as a defining trait of their character?
questions.explanation: The text states that the dhīra-śānta uses discrimination as a central quality.

questions.question: Alongside being peaceful, tolerant, and discriminatory, which quality is explicitly noted as defining the dhīra-śānta?
questions.explanation: The text lists modesty as one of the key qualities possessed by the dhīra-śānta.

2.1.234.verse_text:
An example:

Speaking about dharma in front of Yudhiṣṭhira, Kṛṣṇa appears like the best of brāhmaṇas, pleasant with modesty. His eyes are moving steadily and are filled with love. He shows unlimited good qualities through His skilful speech.

questions.question: When Kṛṣṇa speaks about dharma in the presence of King Yudhiṣṭhira, to which esteemed social class is His demeanor compared?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that while speaking about dharma before Yudhiṣṭhira, Kṛṣṇa appears like the best of brāhmaṇas.

questions.question: What specific quality of character does Kṛṣṇa exhibit while appearing as a brāhmaṇa before Yudhiṣṭhira?
questions.explanation: The text describes Kṛṣṇa as being pleasant with modesty during this interaction.

questions.question: How are Kṛṣṇa's eyes described as He speaks to Yudhiṣṭhira about dharma?
options: They are moving steadily and filled with love
They are closed in deep meditation
They are red from suppressed anger
questions.correct_answer: They are moving steadily and filled with love
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that Kṛṣṇa's eyes are moving steadily and are filled with love while He discusses dharma.

questions.question: Through what medium does Kṛṣṇa manifest His unlimited good qualities during His conversation with Yudhiṣṭhira?
questions.explanation: The verse notes that Kṛṣṇa shows unlimited good qualities specifically through His skilful speech.

2.1.235.verse_text: The learned glorify Yudhiṣṭhira and others as being dhīra-śanta.

questions.question: Which specific classification of heroes is attributed to personalities like King Yudhiṣṭhira by learned scholars?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that the learned glorify Yudhiṣṭhira and others as being dhīra-śānta.

questions.question: Besides King Yudhiṣṭhira, what is the nature of the group of personalities described as possessing the dhīra-śānta quality?
options: They are described as being among 'others' who share these saintly characteristics.
They are exclusively defined as celestial kings living in the higher planetary systems.
They are defined as being those who have never encountered the Supreme Lord.
They are those who have abandoned all social responsibilities for the forest.
questions.correct_answer: They are described as being among 'others' who share these saintly characteristics.
questions.explanation: The text mentions Yudhiṣṭhira 'and others' to indicate that this category includes other similar personalities.

questions.question: According to the learned experts, what is the primary demeanor of one who is categorized as dhīra-śānta?
options: They are characterized by intense anger and a proud nature.
They are known for being peaceful, steady, and saintly.
They are defined by their playful, light-hearted behavior.
They are known for being fickle and highly deceptive.
questions.correct_answer: They are known for being peaceful, steady, and saintly.
questions.explanation: The term dhīra-śānta describes a hero who is peaceful (śānta) and steady or sober (dhīra).

questions.question: Who is the primary authority mentioned in the text for establishing the identity of those who are dhīra-śānta?
questions.explanation: The verse begins by stating 'The learned glorify', identifying them as the ones who recognize and classify these heroes.

2.1.236.verse_text: The wise call dhīroddhata (haughty) the person who shows envy, pride, deceit, anger, fickleness, and boastful nature.

questions.question: Which specific term do the learned authorities utilize to describe an individual who exhibits the qualities of envy, pride, deceit, and anger?
questions.explanation: The verse defines a person characterized by envy, pride, deceit, and anger as dhīroddhata.

questions.question: Which of these traits is explicitly identified in the definition of a dhīroddhata personality?
questions.explanation: Mātsarya (envy) is one of the six specific defects listed for the dhīroddhata personality.

questions.question: Besides envy, pride, and deceit, which other trait is mentioned as a defining characteristic of the dhīroddhata nature?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly identifies kopa (anger) along with fickleness and a boastful nature as traits of the dhīroddhata person.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the 'fickle' nature attributed to the dhīroddhata personality?
questions.explanation: Fickleness (capalatā) is explicitly listed as a core feature of the dhīroddhata individual.

questions.question: When characterizing the dhīroddhata personality, the scriptures mention a 'boastful nature'. Which term indicates this haughty tendency?
questions.explanation: The verse describes the dhīroddhata as having a boastful, haughty nature.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the six traits cited as defining the dhīroddhata person?
questions.explanation: The six traits mentioned are envy, pride, deceit, anger, fickleness, and a boastful nature; compassion is not among these specific characteristics.

2.1.237.verse_text:
An example:

O sinner! King of the Yavanas! O frog! Today, being foiled, make your residence in the corner of a dark hole. The black snake called Kṛṣṇa is waiting there alert to catch you. Just by glancing casually upwards, I have turned the vessel of the universe to ashes.

questions.question: When the speaker in this dramatic utterance addresses the King of the Yavanas as a 'frog,' what specific command is given regarding his residence?
options: To flee to the snowy mountain peaks
To hide in the corner of a dark hole
To dwell within the depths of the ocean
To remain in the open field of battle
questions.correct_answer: To hide in the corner of a dark hole
questions.explanation: The speaker commands the King of the Yavanas to make his residence in the corner of a dark hole.

questions.question: In the dramatic declaration warning the King of the Yavanas, what creature is specifically described as being alert and waiting in the hole?
questions.explanation: The speaker identifies Kṛṣṇa as a black snake waiting in the hole to catch the Yavana King.

questions.question: What is the result of the speaker glancing casually upwards in this particular display of prideful power?
options: The destruction of the demonic armies
The turning of the vessel of the universe to ashes
The awakening of the sleeping demigods
questions.correct_answer: The turning of the vessel of the universe to ashes
questions.explanation: The speaker claims that by glancing casually upwards, they have turned the vessel of the universe to ashes.

questions.question: How does the speaker describe the current state of the King of the Yavanas at the beginning of the address?
questions.explanation: The speaker addresses the Yavana King as one who is currently foiled.

2.1.238.verse_text: The learned call persons like Bhīma dhīroddhata.

questions.question: Which specific category of hero in the classification of devotional mellows is exemplified by the personality of Bhīma?
questions.explanation: The scriptural definition identifies the hero Bhīma as belonging to the classification of dhīroddhata.

questions.question: In the categorization of various hero types, who is explicitly mentioned as an example of the dhīroddhata temperament?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly names Bhīma as the archetype for the dhīroddhata personality.

questions.question: Regarding the classification of heroes, which term do the learned authorities use to describe the nature of Bhīma?
questions.explanation: It is established by the learned that the specific technical designation for Bhīma's hero-type is dhīroddhata.

2.1.239.verse_text: Although qualities such as envy mentioned in verse 236 appear to be faults, they should be regarded as good qualities in Kṛṣṇa, because they are befitting certain pastimes.

questions.question: When observing traits like envy in the pastimes of the Supreme Lord, how should a devotee characterize these apparent faults?
options: As material remnants that must be purified
As good qualities befitting His specific pastimes
As illusory displays meant to test the devotee
As characteristics of His internal potency only
questions.correct_answer: As good qualities befitting His specific pastimes
questions.explanation: The text clarifies that qualities which usually seem like faults are actually good qualities in Kṛṣṇa because they suit His pastimes.

questions.question: What is the primary reason why traits that are normally considered flaws, such as envy, are viewed as divine in the context of Kṛṣṇa's activities?
options: Because He is beyond the influence of the three modes of nature
Because they are essential to His display of human-like beauty
Because they are perfectly befitting the nature of His pastimes
Because they exist only in the minds of the conditioned observers
questions.correct_answer: Because they are perfectly befitting the nature of His pastimes
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that such qualities are regarded as good qualities in Kṛṣṇa because they befit certain pastimes.

questions.question: Which of the following is specifically cited as an example of a trait that appears to be a fault but is actually a good quality in Kṛṣṇa?
questions.explanation: The text specifically mentions envy as a quality that, while appearing as a fault, is a good quality in Kṛṣṇa's pastimes.

questions.question: In the science of rasa, how does the position of Kṛṣṇa regarding ordinary faults differ from that of conditioned souls?
options: Kṛṣṇa's faults are considered ornaments because they befit His pastimes
Kṛṣṇa possesses no faults while souls possess many
Kṛṣṇa's faults are purely external and artificial
Kṛṣṇa uses faults only to punish the demons
questions.correct_answer: Kṛṣṇa's faults are considered ornaments because they befit His pastimes
questions.explanation: The verse confirms that what appears to be a fault in Kṛṣṇa is actually a positive attribute serving the context of His lila.

2.1.240.verse_text:
Another example:

Making the low-hanging, water-filled clouds disperse, swinging a frightful trunk, with terrifying trumpeting sounds, I, the monstrous, irrepressible elephant called Kṛṣṇa, destroyer of opponents, have arrived! Flee from the battlefield, O deer called Śrīdāma!

questions.question: In the dramatic address delivered by the Supreme Lord in His pastimes, what specific atmospheric phenomenon does He claim to disperse with His presence?
options: The dense illusions of the material world
Swirling dust storms on the Yamuna banks
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly mentions the Lord dispersing low-hanging, water-filled clouds as part of His playful declaration.

questions.question: When Kṛṣṇa assumes the identity of a monstrous elephant in this pastime, what specific physical action does He perform to display His terrifying nature?
options: Trampling the ground with all four feet
Bellowing a roar that shakes the earth
questions.explanation: The text describes the Lord's play as an elephant, specifically noting Him swinging a frightful trunk.

questions.question: Beyond the visual display of an elephant, what auditory characteristic does Kṛṣṇa exhibit to instill fear in His opponents?
options: The sound of a thunderous war-drum
questions.explanation: The verse describes the elephant-like Kṛṣṇa producing terrifying trumpeting sounds.

questions.question: In the context of this playful elephant pastime, how does Kṛṣṇa identify His role in relation to those who stand against Him?
questions.explanation: Kṛṣṇa explicitly calls Himself the destroyer of opponents within the metaphor of the monstrous elephant.

questions.question: In this playful exchange, which specific friend of Kṛṣṇa is addressed and compared to a deer?
questions.explanation: Kṛṣṇa playfully calls out to his friend Śrīdāma, addressing him as a deer who should flee the battlefield.

questions.question: To what animal does Kṛṣṇa metaphorically compare Śrīdāma during this sportive interaction?
questions.explanation: Kṛṣṇa playfully commands Śrīdāma, whom He refers to as a deer, to flee the battlefield.

2.1.241.verse_text: Some of the qualities listed above are contradictory to each other. Though contrary, their existence is not impossible in Kṛṣṇa, because of His unrestricted powers.

questions.question: Regarding the divine qualities of the Supreme Lord Kṛṣṇa, what is the observation made about their internal nature?
options: They are contradictory to each other
They are limited by human logic
questions.correct_answer: They are contradictory to each other
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that some of the qualities listed are contradictory to each other.

questions.question: What allows the seemingly contradictory qualities to coexist harmoniously within the personality of Kṛṣṇa?
options: The influence of His external energy
The specific arrangement of His pastimes
questions.explanation: The text confirms that the existence of contrary qualities in Kṛṣṇa is possible because of His unrestricted powers.

questions.question: When considering the presence of opposing attributes within Kṛṣṇa, how does the scripture characterize their manifestation?
options: It is a result of His internal confusion
It is an illusion created for the senses
questions.explanation: Though the qualities may appear contrary, the text states their existence in Kṛṣṇa is not impossible.

2.1.242.verse_text:
This is illustrated in a statement from the Kūrma Purāṇa:

The Lord is not gross, nor is He subtle. But He is both gross and subtle. He is without color, but is blackish with red at the edges of His eyes. By His power He possesses contrary qualities.

questions.question: Regarding the transcendental form of the Lord as described in the Kūrma Purāṇa, what is stated about His gross and subtle nature?
options: He is exclusively gross and never subtle
He is neither gross nor subtle, yet simultaneously both
He is entirely subtle and lacks any gross manifestation
He is strictly gross and manifests subtle qualities only upon request
questions.correct_answer: He is neither gross nor subtle, yet simultaneously both
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states the Lord is not gross, nor is He subtle, yet He is both gross and subtle.

questions.question: What specific physical characteristic is mentioned regarding the Lord's eyes in the Kūrma Purāṇa?
options: They are golden with blue edges
They are blackish with red at the edges
They are white with dark edges
They are entirely reddish in hue
questions.correct_answer: They are blackish with red at the edges
questions.explanation: The verse describes the Lord as being blackish with red at the edges of His eyes.

questions.question: How does the Lord possess contradictory qualities such as being without color yet appearing blackish?
options: Through the influence of material nature
As a result of past karmic reactions
Because of the limitations of the observer
questions.explanation: The verse confirms that by His power the Lord possesses contrary qualities.

questions.question: Which ancient scripture serves as the authority for the description of the Lord's contradictory qualities in this context?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly references a statement from the Kūrma Purāṇa.

2.1.243.verse_text: One should never find any fault in the Lord. Though the qualities are contrary, He can resolve them all completely.

questions.question: What is the primary instruction regarding how a devotee should view the transcendental qualities of the Lord?
options: One should analyze them through logical argument
One should never find any fault in the Lord
One should categorize them into material and spiritual
One should wait for higher realization to accept them
questions.correct_answer: One should never find any fault in the Lord
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly commands that one should never find any fault in the Lord, despite the appearance of contradictory qualities.

questions.question: Regarding the seemingly contradictory qualities displayed by the Supreme Lord, what is His unique capacity?
options: He keeps them separate to avoid confusion
He hides them from the conditioned souls
He can resolve them all completely
He requires the assistance of His internal potency to balance them
questions.correct_answer: He can resolve them all completely
questions.explanation: The text states that although the qualities appear contrary, the Lord has the inherent capacity to resolve them all completely.

questions.question: What is the reason a devotee must be careful not to find fault in the Lord's activities or qualities?
options: Because the Lord can resolve all apparent contradictions completely
Because the Lord is beyond the influence of time
Because the Lord is the creator of the material world
Because the Lord is the source of all the Vedas
questions.correct_answer: Because the Lord can resolve all apparent contradictions completely
questions.explanation: The text implies that fault-finding is inappropriate because the Lord possesses the power to harmonize even contradictory qualities.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the nature of the qualities possessed by the Lord as mentioned in the teaching?
options: They are difficult for the Lord to manage
questions.explanation: The verse acknowledges that the qualities of the Lord appear to be 'contrary', yet He resolves them.

2.1.244-245.verse_text:
This is confirmed in the Mahā-varāha Purāṇa:

All of the bodies of the Lord are eternal, and appear repeatedly in the material world. They are devoid of decrease and increase. They are never born of matter. All the bodies have the very nature of the highest bliss, are pure knowledge, are full of all good qualities, and are devoid of all faults.

questions.question: What is the inherent nature of the Lord's divine bodies in relation to their existence within the material world?
options: They appear repeatedly and are eternal
They are born from the elements of nature
They undergo gradual growth and decay
They manifest only once as unique entities
questions.correct_answer: They appear repeatedly and are eternal
questions.explanation: The scriptural evidence confirms that all bodies of the Lord are eternal and appear repeatedly in the material world.

questions.question: Regarding the Lord's manifestation, what can be stated about the occurrence of decrease or increase in His transcendental body?
options: They fluctuate according to the time of appearance
They are completely devoid of any such change
They increase as the Lord performs pastimes
They decrease when He resides in the material realm
questions.correct_answer: They are completely devoid of any such change
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the bodies of the Lord are devoid of decrease and increase.

questions.question: What is the origin of the Lord's body according to the descriptions found in the Mahā-varāha Purāṇa?
options: It is born of material elements
It is never born of matter
It is created by the modes of material nature
It is produced from the energy of the jivas
questions.correct_answer: It is never born of matter
questions.explanation: The text clearly declares that the bodies of the Lord are never born of matter.

questions.question: What is described as the essential nature of the Lord's divine form?
questions.explanation: The scripture states that all the bodies of the Lord have the very nature of the highest bliss.

questions.question: Beyond being blissful, what constitutes the fundamental substance of the Lord's form?
questions.explanation: The text confirms that the Lord's bodies are pure knowledge.

questions.question: What is the status of good qualities in the manifestations of the Lord?
options: The bodies are full of all good qualities
questions.correct_answer: The bodies are full of all good qualities
questions.explanation: The text affirms that all the bodies of the Lord are full of all good qualities.

questions.question: How are the bodies of the Lord characterized in relation to faults?
options: They are devoid of all faults
They are susceptible to external influence
They are faultless only in the spiritual world
questions.correct_answer: They are devoid of all faults
questions.explanation: The scriptural passage states that the Lord's bodies are devoid of all faults.

2.1.246.verse_text:
It is also confirmed in a Vaiṣṇava-tantra:

The form of the Lord is without the eighteen great faults, is endowed with all powers and is the very essence (svarūpa) of existence, knowledge and bliss.

questions.question: According to the Vaiṣṇava-tantra, how many distinct great faults are completely absent from the transcendental form of the Supreme Lord?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states the Lord's form is without the eighteen great faults.

questions.question: Which of the following descriptions characterizes the transcendental form of the Lord as mentioned in the Vaiṣṇava-tantra?
options: Dependent upon the three modes of nature
questions.explanation: The verse states the form of the Lord is endowed with all powers.

questions.question: The essence or svarūpa of the Supreme Lord's form is described as a combination of which three transcendental qualities?
questions.explanation: The verse defines the Lord's form as the essence of existence (sat), knowledge (cit), and bliss (ananda).

questions.question: In the context of the Lord's transcendental nature, what does the term 'svarūpa' refer to within the Vaiṣṇava-tantra?
options: His external expansions for cosmic creation
The very essence of His existence, knowledge, and bliss
The conditioned state of the living entity
questions.correct_answer: The very essence of His existence, knowledge, and bliss
questions.explanation: The verse identifies the Lord's form as the svarupa of existence, knowledge, and bliss.

2.1.247-248.verse_text:
The eighteen great faults are mentioned in the Viṣṇu-yāmala:

The eighteen faults are bewilderment, sleep, error, material attachment without prema, material lust which brings suffering, fickleness, intoxication, envy, violence, exhaustion, toil, lying, anger, hankering, worry, absorption in worldly affairs, prejudice, and dependency on others.

questions.question: Among the eighteen great faults that are absent in the Supreme Lord, which of the following is described as a state of bewilderment or confusion?
questions.explanation: The text lists 'bhrama' (bewilderment/error) as one of the eighteen great faults that do not exist in the Supreme Lord.

questions.question: Which of the following is explicitly listed as one of the eighteen material faults that obstruct pure devotional service due to its nature of being a source of suffering?
questions.explanation: Material lust, which brings suffering, is specifically identified as one of the eighteen great faults.

questions.question: In the list of eighteen great faults, which fault is characterized by a state of exhaustion or fatigue?
questions.explanation: Klama is the Sanskrit term for exhaustion or fatigue, which is listed as one of the eighteen great faults.

questions.question: Which of the following is identified as one of the eighteen great faults involving an envious nature?
questions.explanation: Matsarya, or envy, is enumerated among the eighteen great faults that are entirely absent in the Supreme Lord.

questions.question: When considering the eighteen great faults, which of these is described as the tendency toward intoxication or pride?
questions.explanation: Mada, or intoxication/pride, is one of the eighteen defects mentioned in the Viṣṇu-yāmala.

questions.question: Which fault in the list of eighteen is specifically described as the tendency to tell lies?
questions.explanation: Anrta, or lying, is listed as one of the eighteen faults that are foreign to the nature of the Supreme Lord.

questions.question: Which of these is one of the eighteen great faults characterized by intense anxiety or worry?
questions.explanation: Cinta, or worry, is one of the eighteen material faults recorded in the Viṣṇu-yāmala.

questions.question: Which of the following is listed among the eighteen great faults and refers to the act of violence or causing harm?
questions.explanation: Himsa, or violence, is one of the eighteen great faults that the Lord is completely devoid of.

questions.question: Within the eighteen faults mentioned in the Viṣṇu-yāmala, what is the term for the fault of being dependent on others?
questions.explanation: Paratantrata, or dependency on others, is listed as one of the eighteen great faults that cannot exist in the Absolute Lord.

questions.question: Which of the following is one of the eighteen great faults that relates to the tendency of becoming fickle or unsteady?
questions.explanation: Capalya, or fickleness, is included in the list of eighteen great faults.

2.1.249.verse_text: Thus, it has been explained that the amount of sweetness in Kṛṣṇa exceeds that of all of the avatāras and the source of avatāras, Mahā-viṣṇu.

questions.question: Regarding the inherent sweetness found in the various manifestations of the Supreme, whose sweetness is explicitly declared to be the greatest?
questions.explanation: The verse confirms that Kṛṣṇa's sweetness is unparalleled and surpasses the sweetness of all other incarnations and their source.

questions.question: In the comparison of devotional sweetness, which specific category of expansions is noted as having less sweetness than Kṛṣṇa?
questions.explanation: The text states that Kṛṣṇa's sweetness exceeds that of all avatāras, without exception.

questions.question: Beyond the various avatāras, who else's sweetness is surpassed by the supreme sweetness of Kṛṣṇa?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly mentions that even the sweetness of Mahā-viṣṇu, who is the source of all other avatāras, is exceeded by Kṛṣṇa.

questions.question: What is the relationship between Mahā-viṣṇu and the various avatāras in the context of the comparison of sweetness?
options: Mahā-viṣṇu is the source of all avatāras
Mahā-viṣṇu is equal to all avatāras
Mahā-viṣṇu is an expansion of the avatāras
questions.correct_answer: Mahā-viṣṇu is the source of all avatāras
questions.explanation: The verse identifies Mahā-viṣṇu specifically as the source of all avatāras, whose sweetness is still surpassed by Kṛṣṇa.

2.1.250.verse_text:
This is explained in the Brahma-saṃhitā:

Mahā-viṣṇu, into whom all the innumerable universes enter and from whom they come forth again simply by His breathing process, is a plenary expansion of Kṛṣṇa. Therefore, I worship Govinda, Kṛṣṇa, the cause of all causes.

questions.question: According to the Brahma-samhita, what is the fundamental relationship between Mahā-viṣṇu and Kṛṣṇa?
options: Mahā-viṣṇu is a plenary expansion of Kṛṣṇa
Mahā-viṣṇu is the original source of Kṛṣṇa
Mahā-viṣṇu and Kṛṣṇa are independent manifestations
Mahā-viṣṇu is an incarnation of an expansion of Kṛṣṇa
questions.correct_answer: Mahā-viṣṇu is a plenary expansion of Kṛṣṇa
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Mahā-viṣṇu is a plenary expansion of Kṛṣṇa.

questions.question: What happens to the innumerable universes in relation to Mahā-viṣṇu during His breathing process?
options: They are purified by His breath
They enter into Him and come forth again
They remain static within His effulgence
questions.correct_answer: They are entering into Him and coming forth again
questions.explanation: The verse describes that the universes enter into Mahā-viṣṇu and come forth from Him simply by the process of His breathing.

questions.question: Which title is used to describe Govinda, Kṛṣṇa, in the final statement of the Brahma-samhita verse cited?
options: The Maintainer of All Living Entities
questions.explanation: The verse concludes by identifying Govinda, Kṛṣṇa, as the cause of all causes.

questions.question: By what specific action does Mahā-viṣṇu facilitate the manifestation and dissolution of the innumerable universes?
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that the movement of universes in and out of Mahā-viṣṇu occurs through His breathing process.

2.1.251-252.verse_text: Excellent qualities of the heart, the embodiments of auspiciousness, are glorified as eight: glory, playfulness, sweetness, auspiciousness, steadiness, strength, beauty and generosity.

questions.question: How many specific excellent qualities of the heart are glorified as the embodiments of auspiciousness according to the teachings of Srila Rupa Gosvami?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly categorizes eight excellent qualities of the heart as embodiments of auspiciousness.

questions.question: Which of the following is explicitly listed as one of the eight excellent qualities of the heart?
questions.explanation: Glory is identified as one of the eight excellent heart qualities alongside playfulness, sweetness, auspiciousness, steadiness, strength, beauty, and generosity.

questions.question: Along with glory, strength, and beauty, which quality completes the list of eight auspicious heart embodiments?
questions.explanation: Playfulness is one of the eight qualities mentioned, alongside glory, sweetness, auspiciousness, steadiness, strength, beauty, and generosity.

questions.question: Which of the following is considered an embodiment of auspiciousness within the context of excellent heart qualities?
questions.explanation: Sweetness is explicitly listed as one of the eight excellent qualities of the heart.

questions.question: In the enumeration of eight excellent qualities of the heart, which of the following is included?
questions.explanation: Steadiness is defined as one of the eight excellent qualities of the heart in this list.

questions.question: Besides strength and generosity, what other quality is categorized as an embodiment of auspiciousness?
questions.explanation: Auspiciousness itself is one of the eight qualities, alongside strength and generosity, that comprise the heart's excellent characteristics.

questions.question: Which of the following is explicitly included as a heart quality among the eight mentioned by Srila Rupa Gosvami?
questions.explanation: Generosity is the final quality in the list of eight excellent heart qualities.

questions.question: Among the list of eight qualities of the heart, which of the following is named as an embodiment of auspiciousness?
questions.explanation: Strength is one of the eight qualities identified as the embodiment of auspiciousness.

questions.question: Which of these is recognized as one of the eight qualities embodying auspiciousness within the heart?
questions.explanation: Beauty is listed among the eight excellent heart qualities that embody auspiciousness.

2.1.253.verse_text: Among the ornaments of auspiciousness, where there is mercy to those who are lower, emulation of those who one regards as greater, as well as courage, enthusiasm, expertise and truth, it is known as śobha (glory).

questions.question: Within the ornament known as śobha, what is the specific attitude one should adopt toward those who are regarded as greater?
questions.explanation: The text defines śobha as including the emulation of those one regards as greater.

questions.question: Which specific virtue is described as being directed toward those who are lower as a characteristic of the ornament of śobha?
questions.explanation: Śobha is characterized by showing mercy to those who are lower in status.

questions.question: Besides mercy, emulation, courage, enthusiasm, and truth, what is the final quality listed as a component of śobha?
questions.explanation: The ornament śobha is explicitly composed of mercy, emulation, courage, enthusiasm, expertise, and truth.

questions.question: How many distinct virtues or qualities are mentioned as constituting the ornament known as śobha?
questions.explanation: The text lists six qualities: mercy, emulation, courage, enthusiasm, expertise, and truth.

questions.question: In the context of the ornament of śobha, which quality refers to the steady dedication to reality or factual consistency?
questions.explanation: Truth is explicitly listed as one of the constituent qualities of śobha.

2.1.254.verse_text:
An example:

Seeing the pain caused by Indra’s rain in Vraja, Kṛṣṇa desired to destroy Svarga, but then, considering Indra and the devatās to be inferior, a wave of compassion rose within Him. Seeing no one equal to Himself as a suitable object of anger, He who is firm in truth then lifted Govardhana, with the desire of giving bliss to His friends.

questions.question: What specifically motivated Kṛṣṇa to lift Govardhana Hill when He observed the distress caused by Indra's torrential rain in Vraja?
options: To personally punish Indra for his pride
To establish His authority as the Supreme Lord
To provide bliss to His dear friends
To demonstrate His physical strength to the cowherd boys
questions.correct_answer: To provide bliss to His dear friends
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Kṛṣṇa lifted Govardhana with the specific desire of giving bliss to His friends.

questions.question: What internal disposition prevented Kṛṣṇa from fully engaging in anger toward Indra during the Govardhana pastime?
options: He considered Indra and the devatas to be inferior
He was too absorbed in the beauty of the Vraja-vasis
He feared disrupting the cosmic order
He wanted to test the faith of the residents of Vraja
questions.correct_answer: He considered Indra and the devatas to be inferior
questions.explanation: The verse explains that a wave of compassion arose in Kṛṣṇa because He considered Indra and the devatas to be inferior.

questions.question: When Kṛṣṇa looked for a suitable target for His anger during the incident of the rains in Vraja, what conclusion did He reach?
options: Indra was worthy of being destroyed
No one was equal to Him
The inhabitants of Svarga were too powerful
questions.correct_answer: No one was equal to Him
questions.explanation: The text mentions that He saw no one equal to Himself as a suitable object of anger, which changed His mood toward compassion.

questions.question: What primary initial desire did Kṛṣṇa have upon witnessing the suffering caused by Indra's rain in Vraja?
options: To initiate a battle with the demigods
To destroy the heavenly realm of Svarga
To flee with the inhabitants of Vraja
To summon the assistance of Balarama
questions.correct_answer: To destroy the heavenly realm of Svarga
questions.explanation: The verse clearly states that upon seeing the pain caused by Indra's rain, Kṛṣṇa desired to destroy Svarga.

questions.question: Which specific virtuous trait is attributed to Kṛṣṇa in the context of lifting Govardhana Hill?
questions.explanation: The text describes Him as 'He who is firm in truth' (satya-pratishtha) when He proceeded to lift the mountain.

2.1.255.verse_text: Where there is a heavy gait, steady gaze like that of a bull, and laughing words, it is called vilāsa (playfulness).

questions.question: When observing the playful demeanor known as vilāsa, which specific physical attribute is characterized by a heavy, deliberate gait?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly defines the characteristics of vilāsa as including a heavy gait.

questions.question: Which animal's gaze is used as the standard of comparison to describe the steady, focused look associated with the state of vilāsa?
questions.explanation: The verse identifies that the steady gaze of vilāsa is likened to that of a bull.

questions.question: Beyond the physical gait and gaze, what specific manner of speech is cited as a hallmark of vilāsa?
questions.explanation: The definition of vilāsa provided in the text includes the characteristic of using laughing words.

questions.question: Which term is used to describe the specific emotional or behavioral display characterized by a heavy gait, a bull-like steady gaze, and laughing words?
questions.explanation: Vilāsa is the technical term defined by these three specific symptomatic characteristics.

2.1.256.verse_text:
An example:

Lotus-eyed Kṛṣṇa, while gazing steadily and boldly at the wrestlers, shaking the earth like an elephant, proud with victory, His words touched with the fragrance of humor, entered the raised arena while sprinkling water on the stage.

questions.question: When the lotus-eyed Lord entered the wrestling arena, what specific comparison was used to describe the way He shook the earth?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Kṛṣṇa shook the earth like an elephant while entering the arena.

questions.question: What was the specific physical action performed by Kṛṣṇa toward the wrestling stage as He entered the arena?
options: He struck it with His club
questions.explanation: The verse notes that Kṛṣṇa entered the arena while sprinkling water on the stage.

questions.question: In the description of Kṛṣṇa entering the wrestling arena, how is His gaze directed toward the wrestlers characterized?
questions.explanation: The verse describes Kṛṣṇa as gazing steadily and boldly at the wrestlers present in the arena.

questions.question: What internal feeling or sentiment accompanied Kṛṣṇa as He entered the arena to face the wrestlers?
questions.explanation: The text characterizes Kṛṣṇa at that moment as being proud with victory.

questions.question: What quality was noted about the words spoken by Kṛṣṇa as He entered the wrestling arena?
options: They were touched with the fragrance of humor
They were filled with divine commands
They were heavy with deep wisdom
They were seasoned with intense anger
questions.correct_answer: They were touched with the fragrance of humor
questions.explanation: The verse mentions that Kṛṣṇa's words were touched with the fragrance of humor.

2.1.257.verse_text: Where there is an expression of desire through actions it is called sweetness.

questions.question: What is the specific term used to describe the manifestation of an inner desire expressed through one's physical actions?
questions.explanation: The text defines that sweetness, or mādhurya, is the expression of desire through physical actions.

questions.question: When a devotee or the Lord reveals their inner longing through the medium of bodily gestures and movements, how is this state categorized within the science of devotion?
questions.explanation: The scriptural definition provided explicitly equates the expression of desire through actions with sweetness, or mādhurya.

questions.question: In the analysis of devotional interactions, if one's hidden intent becomes visible through active behavior, what does this specific quality represent?
questions.explanation: The verse defines that where there is an expression of desire through actions, it is recognized as sweetness (mādhurya).

questions.question: Which of the following serves as the primary instrument for manifesting the quality known as mādhurya?
questions.explanation: The definition states that sweetness is expressed specifically through actions.

2.1.258.verse_text: While Kṛṣṇa was sitting on the bank of the Yamunā, tarrying there on the pretext of making a long garland of golden kadamba flowers, Rādhā arrived at a ghāṭa on the river. He threw a glance from the corner of His eye at the doe-eyed Rādhā.

questions.question: What was the specific setting or location where the Supreme Lord Kṛṣṇa was positioned while waiting for Rādhā?
options: The bank of the Yamunā river
The base of a Govardhana hill
The outskirts of the Nandagrāma village
questions.correct_answer: The bank of the Yamunā river
questions.explanation: The text states that Kṛṣṇa was sitting on the bank of the Yamunā.

questions.question: When Rādhā appeared at the ghāṭa, what activity was Kṛṣṇa engaging in as a pretext to tarry by the Yamunā?
options: Making a long garland of golden kadamba flowers
Sorting through piles of fresh lotus blossoms
Weaving a crown of forest peacock feathers
questions.correct_answer: Making a long garland of golden kadamba flowers
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly mentions He was tarrying there on the pretext of making a long garland of golden kadamba flowers.

questions.question: Which specific physical feature of Rādhā is highlighted as She arrived at the ghāṭa?
questions.explanation: The text refers to Rādhā as 'doe-eyed' when describing her arrival.

questions.question: In what manner did Kṛṣṇa acknowledge the arrival of the doe-eyed Rādhā at the river ghāṭa?
options: By calling out Her name softly
By throwing a glance from the corner of His eye
By standing up to greet Her with open arms
By playing a special melody on His flute
questions.correct_answer: By throwing a glance from the corner of His eye
questions.explanation: The verse describes that He threw a glance from the corner of His eye at the doe-eyed Rādhā.

questions.question: What color or type of flowers was Kṛṣṇa using to construct His garland while waiting at the riverbank?
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that the garland being constructed by Kṛṣṇa was composed of golden kadamba flowers.

2.1.259.verse_text: Being the object of faith for the world is called “having auspiciousness.”

questions.question: What is the technical term used to describe the state of being the primary object of faith for the entire world?
questions.explanation: The verse defines the condition of being the object of faith for the world as 'having auspiciousness'.

questions.question: In the context of the qualities of the Lord, what specific definition is given for the term 'auspiciousness'?
options: Being the object of faith for the world
The capacity to perform miraculous pastimes
questions.correct_answer: Being the object of faith for the world
questions.explanation: It is established that the Lord being the recipient of the world's faith is what constitutes the quality known as auspiciousness.

questions.question: What is the essential nature of the quality defined as 'auspiciousness' within the devotional understanding of the Lord?
options: The internal potency of the Lord
The Lord as the central focus of universal faith
The mercy granted to the conditioned souls
questions.correct_answer: The Lord as the central focus of universal faith
questions.explanation: The verse identifies auspiciousness specifically with the Lord being the object of faith for all beings in the world.

2.1.260.verse_text:
An example:

Because there is no sense of injustice in the Lord, even the demons leave their doors open. Because they have faith that Kṛṣṇa is their protector, the devatās indulge in play without worry. Understanding that He is the witness and thus knows their bhakti, those offering obeisances (devotees) have given up anxiety. O supporter of the universe! Who does not have faith in Your lotus feet?

questions.question: Regarding the Lord's nature, why do the demons keep their doors open without fear of injustice?
options: Because He is the ultimate punisher of all beings
Because there is no sense of injustice in the Lord
Because they seek to hide their wealth from the devatas
Because they are forced by the influence of time
questions.correct_answer: Because there is no sense of injustice in the Lord
questions.explanation: The verse states that because there is no sense of injustice in the Lord, even the demons leave their doors open.

questions.question: Why are the devatas able to indulge in play without any worry or anxiety?
options: Because they possess infinite mystical powers
Because they have faith that Krsna is their protector
Because they are residents of the heavenly planets
Because they have conquered the influence of maya
questions.correct_answer: Because they have faith that Krsna is their protector
questions.explanation: The text explains that the devatas indulge in play without worry because they have faith that Krsna is their protector.

questions.question: What understanding allows those offering obeisances to the Lord to completely abandon all anxiety?
options: Understanding that He is the witness of their bhakti
Understanding that He is the source of all material creation
Understanding that He resides within the hearts of all living beings
Understanding that He is the destroyer of all sinful reactions
questions.correct_answer: Understanding that He is the witness of their bhakti
questions.explanation: The verse notes that devotees have given up anxiety because they understand that the Lord is the witness who knows their bhakti.

questions.question: In the concluding address of the verse, how is the Lord invoked regarding His relationship to the material world?
options: O master of all mystic perfection
questions.explanation: The verse addresses the Lord as the supporter of the universe when asking who does not have faith in His lotus feet.

2.1.261.verse_text: To remain fixed in one’s duties even though they are filled with obstacles is called steadiness.

questions.question: What is the specific definition of steadiness (dhairya) in the context of one's devotional duties?
options: Remaining fixed in one's duties despite the presence of obstacles
The ability to tolerate the insults of the envious
Refraining from engaging in sinful activities
Constantly remembering the pastimes of the Lord
questions.correct_answer: Remaining fixed in one's duties despite the presence of obstacles
questions.explanation: Steadiness is defined as the quality of remaining steadfast in one's prescribed duties even when they are beset by various impediments.

questions.question: If a devotee continues to perform their devotional service while facing significant obstructions, what specific quality are they exhibiting?
questions.explanation: The characteristic of maintaining one's performance of duties in the face of obstacles is identified as dhairya, or steadiness.

questions.question: Which term describes the state of a devotee who does not abandon their prescribed activities when challenged by external or internal hindrances?
questions.explanation: Dhairya represents the firm commitment to one's duties regardless of the obstacles encountered during that process.

questions.question: When considering the path of bhakti, what is the essential characteristic of one who acts with 'steadiness'?
options: A total lack of external interference
A constant state of emotional ecstasy
Fixity in duty regardless of obstacles
questions.correct_answer: Fixity in duty regardless of obstacles
questions.explanation: Steadiness is specifically defined by the quality of remaining fixed in one's duties, even when those duties are filled with difficulties.

2.1.262.verse_text: Even though His act was obstructed by Śiva with his trident and Bāṇa’s mother[176] who had appeared without clothing, Mukunda cut off the arms of Bāṇāsura.

questions.question: When Mukunda performed the feat of cutting off the arms of Bāṇāsura, which powerful divine figure attempted to obstruct His actions using a trident?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Lord Śiva attempted to obstruct Mukunda's action with his trident.

questions.question: Which divine weapon did Śiva wield in his unsuccessful attempt to impede the Lord's determination to cut off the arms of Bāṇāsura?
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that Śiva used his trident to obstruct the Lord's act.

questions.question: Besides Lord Śiva, who else appeared in a state of undress to obstruct Mukunda as He engaged with Bāṇāsura?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Bāṇāsura's mother as the individual who appeared without clothing to obstruct the Lord.

questions.question: In the narration of Mukunda's victory over Bāṇāsura, what specific physical appearance was noted regarding Bāṇāsura's mother during the confrontation?
options: She was adorned in gold ornaments
questions.explanation: The verse describes Bāṇāsura's mother as having appeared without clothing in her attempt to obstruct the Lord.

questions.question: What was the final outcome for Bāṇāsura at the hands of Mukunda, despite the divine obstructions presented by Śiva and others?
questions.explanation: The verse concludes that despite the obstructions, Mukunda successfully cut off the arms of Bāṇāsura.

2.1.263.verse_text: The learned say that the ability to enter into the hearts of all others is called influence (tejas).

questions.question: What is the technical definition given for the spiritual quality known as tejas or influence?
options: The capacity to reside within the hearts of all living entities
The ability to assume the universal form of the Supreme Lord
The power to destroy the enemies of the devotees
The talent to manifest infinite opulences simultaneously
questions.correct_answer: The capacity to reside within the hearts of all living entities
questions.explanation: The text explicitly defines tejas as the ability to enter into the hearts of all others.

questions.question: In the study of devotional qualities, which term describes the specific power of entering into the hearts of others?
questions.explanation: The verse identifies tejas as the specific terminology used by the learned for the influence that allows one to enter the hearts of others.

questions.question: Who defines the quality of tejas, or the ability to enter the hearts of all others, as a recognized spiritual characteristic?
questions.explanation: The verse begins by stating that the learned categorize this specific power as influence or tejas.

questions.question: How do the learned describe the spiritual influence known as tejas?
options: As the power to control the material elements
As the capacity to enter into the hearts of all others
As the ability to manifest various forms of the Lord
As the strength to defeat all challengers in a wrestling match
questions.correct_answer: As the capacity to enter into the hearts of all others
questions.explanation: According to the verse, tejas is defined as the ability to enter into the hearts of all others.

2.1.264.verse_text:
An example from the Tenth Canto:

The various groups of people in the arena regarded Kṛṣṇa in different ways when He entered it with His elder brother. The wrestlers saw Kṛṣṇa as a lightning bolt, the men of Mathurā as the best of males, the women as Cupid in person, the cowherd men as their relative, the impious rulers as a chastiser, His parents as their child, the King of the Bhojas as death, the unintelligent as the Supreme Lord’s universal form, the yogīs as the Absolute Truth and the Vṛṣṇis as their supreme worshipable Deity. SB 10.43.17

questions.question: When Kṛṣṇa entered the wrestling arena with Balarāma, how did the professional wrestlers perceive Him?
options: As the Supreme Lord's universal form
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the wrestlers saw Kṛṣṇa as a lightning bolt.

questions.question: How did the men of Mathurā view Kṛṣṇa upon His arrival at the arena?
questions.explanation: The verse notes that the men of Mathurā saw Kṛṣṇa as the best of males.

questions.question: In what form did the women of Mathurā perceive Kṛṣṇa when He walked into the arena?
questions.explanation: The text describes the perception of the women as seeing Kṛṣṇa as Cupid in person.

questions.question: How did the cowherd men perceive Kṛṣṇa as He entered the wrestling arena?
questions.explanation: The cowherd men viewed Kṛṣṇa, their beloved, specifically as their relative.

questions.question: What was the perspective of the impious rulers when they looked upon Kṛṣṇa at the arena?
options: As the Supreme Lord's universal form
questions.explanation: The text mentions that the impious rulers saw Him as a chastiser.

questions.question: How did Kṛṣṇa's parents perceive Him when He arrived at the wrestling arena?
questions.explanation: The verse clarifies that His parents saw Him as their child.

questions.question: How did the King of the Bhojas (Kaṃsa) perceive Kṛṣṇa during the entrance into the arena?
questions.explanation: The King of the Bhojas was terrified, seeing Kṛṣṇa as death itself.

questions.question: How did the unintelligent people perceive Kṛṣṇa upon His entry into the arena?
options: As the Supreme Lord's universal form
questions.correct_answer: As the Supreme Lord's universal form
questions.explanation: The text states that those who were unintelligent saw Him as the Supreme Lord's universal form.

questions.question: How did the yogīs perceive Kṛṣṇa as He walked into the arena?
questions.explanation: The yogīs, focused on the impersonal aspect, perceived Kṛṣṇa as the Absolute Truth.

questions.question: How did the Vṛṣṇis perceive Kṛṣṇa when He entered the arena?
questions.explanation: The Vṛṣṇis viewed Kṛṣṇa as their supreme worshipable Deity.

2.1.265.verse_text:
Another definition of tejas:

The intelligent say that tejas means intolerance of offense (since another meaning of tejas is impatience and fierce opposition).

questions.question: What is the specific definition of the term tejas provided in the context of devotional characteristics?
options: The brilliance of a glowing lamp
The heat emitted by the sun
A state of deep meditative absorption
questions.explanation: The text explicitly defines tejas as intolerance of offense, noting it carries the sense of impatience and fierce opposition.

questions.question: Beyond the concept of impatience, what quality is synonymous with the term tejas in its application to a devotee's nature?
questions.explanation: The text clarifies that tejas is characterized by intolerance of offense, which manifests as impatience and fierce opposition.

questions.question: In the context of the qualities of a devotee, what does the term tejas specifically indicate regarding an external transgression or offense?
questions.explanation: The scriptural definition provided identifies tejas as the quality of being intolerant of any offense committed.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the psychological and spiritual state inherent in the quality of tejas?
questions.explanation: The text notes that tejas implies impatience and fierce opposition toward offenses.

2.1.266.verse_text:
An example:

When the hater of the whole universe, angry Kaṃsa, loudly beckons to Nanda and Vasudeva, with the desire to give them punishment, Kṛṣṇa throws a glance like a messenger sent to the unchaste woman called death for the demons, and ascends the arena with a desire to play.

questions.question: When the angry King Kamsa summons Nanda and Vasudeva into the arena, what is his primary intent toward them?
options: To engage them in a friendly debate
questions.explanation: The text states that Kamsa beckons them with the desire to give them punishment.

questions.question: How does the text characterize the disposition of Kamsa as he calls for Nanda and Vasudeva?
options: As a lover of the Lord
As a hater of the whole universe
questions.correct_answer: As a hater of the whole universe
questions.explanation: Kamsa is explicitly described as the hater of the whole universe.

questions.question: When Krsna glances at Kamsa, the text compares His look to a messenger being sent to which specific entity?
questions.explanation: Krsna's glance is compared to a messenger sent to the unchaste woman called death, specifically for the demons.

questions.question: With what internal desire does Krsna ascend the wrestling arena in the presence of Kamsa?
options: A desire to display His majesty
A desire to initiate a formal battle
questions.explanation: The text describes that Krsna ascends the arena with a desire to play.

2.1.267.verse_text: Where there are activities of explicit conjugal nature it is known as lalita, voluptuousness.

questions.question: What is the specific technical term used to describe those devotional activities that manifest an explicit conjugal nature?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly defines activities of an explicit conjugal nature as being known as lalita.

questions.question: How is the quality of voluptuousness, or lalita, characterized within the context of devotional expression?
options: As activities of an explicit conjugal nature
As the highest form of dasya-rasa
As a secondary stage of vatsalya-bhava
As the meditative state of santa-rasa
questions.correct_answer: As activities of an explicit conjugal nature
questions.explanation: Lalita is defined by the presence of explicit conjugal activities.

questions.question: In the categorization of devotional mellows, which term identifies the voluptuous or explicit conjugal dimension of behavior?
questions.explanation: Lalita is the specific term provided in the text to identify activities of an explicit conjugal nature.

questions.question: If a devotee observes activities that are distinctly voluptuous and of an explicit conjugal nature, which category of expression is this?
questions.explanation: The text identifies voluptuousness, or activities of an explicit conjugal nature, as lalita.

2.1.268.verse_text:
An example:

The king of rasa, Kṛṣṇa is joyfully drawing makarīs on the bud-like breasts of Radha with His right hand using a steady mind. When Ariṣṭāsura roars harshly with pride, Kṛṣṇa, while laughing at him as goose bumps rise on His flesh, ties His belt with His left hand.

questions.question: Which specific ornament or figure is Sri Krishna playfully drawing upon the breasts of Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Krishna is drawing makaris on the bud-like breasts of Radha.

questions.question: Which hand does Sri Krishna utilize to draw the decorative figures upon the chest of Srimati Radharani?
options: He uses a brush held in His right hand
questions.explanation: It is mentioned that He is drawing with His right hand while maintaining a steady mind.

questions.question: Whose prideful and harsh roaring interrupts the intimate pastimes of the Divine Couple?
questions.explanation: The verse identifies Aristasura as the one roaring harshly with pride.

questions.question: When faced with the disturbance caused by the demon, what physical symptom of ecstasy manifests on the body of Sri Krishna?
questions.explanation: The text describes that goose bumps rise on Krishna's flesh as He laughs at the demon.

questions.question: While confronting the threat, which hand does Sri Krishna use to adjust and tie His belt?
options: He does not tie His belt during this time
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that Krishna ties His belt with His left hand while laughing at the demon.

questions.question: What is the emotional expression of Sri Krishna as He reacts to the prideful roar of the demon?
questions.explanation: The text states that Krishna is laughing at Aristasura as the demon roars.

2.1.269.verse_text: Generosity is glorified as the willingness to offer to another person even one’s soul.

questions.question: What sublime quality is defined by the absolute willingness to offer even one’s own soul to another person?
questions.explanation: Generosity is specifically glorified as the willingness to offer one's own soul to another person.

questions.question: When considering the definition of generosity within the realm of devotion, what is the ultimate extent of the offering mentioned?
questions.explanation: The verse identifies the pinnacle of generosity as the willingness to offer even one's soul.

questions.question: How is the act of offering one's soul to another categorized in the science of devotional service?
options: As a type of formal worship
questions.explanation: Generosity is explicitly identified as the willingness to offer one's soul to another.

questions.question: Which of the following descriptions accurately characterizes the supreme standard of generosity taught by Rupa Gosvami?
options: Giving up all material desires for the sake of the Lord
Offering one’s own soul to another person
Sharing knowledge of the scriptures with the inquisitive
Distributing prasada to all living entities
questions.correct_answer: Offering one’s own soul to another person
questions.explanation: The verse defines the act of offering one's soul as the standard for glorifying generosity.

2.1.270.verse_text: Say if there is anyone more magnanimous than the Supreme Lord, who offers even His soul to the destitute and nondescript?

questions.question: What is the defining mark of the Supreme Lord's magnanimity toward the destitute and nondescript?
options: He grants them liberation from material existence
He offers even His own soul to them
He bestows the wealth of Vaikuntha
questions.correct_answer: He offers even His own soul to them
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly asks who could be more magnanimous than the Lord, specifically noting that He offers His very soul to the lowly.

questions.question: To which specific group of people is the Supreme Lord's magnanimity uniquely directed in this context?
options: The great sages and learned scholars
The demigods in the higher planetary systems
questions.explanation: The text highlights that the Lord offers His soul specifically to those described as destitute and nondescript.

questions.question: How does the author rhetorically characterize the extent of the Supreme Lord's magnanimity?
options: By questioning if anyone else possesses such magnanimity
By comparing Him to the greatest of sages
By measuring His actions against the Vedic scriptures
By claiming all other beings are inherently magnanimous
questions.correct_answer: By questioning if anyone else possesses such magnanimity
questions.explanation: The verse uses a rhetorical question to challenge the reader to find anyone more magnanimous than the Lord who offers His soul.

2.1.271.verse_text: Although these eight qualities were previously discussed, because they are somewhat remarkable, they have again been described in a separate category.

questions.question: Why are the eight specific transcendental qualities of the Lord re-presented in a distinct, separate category despite their prior mention?
options: Because they possess a unique and remarkable nature
Because they are essential for the initiation of a devotee
Because they were previously described only in the context of the material world
Because the author wished to lengthen the text for clarity
questions.correct_answer: Because they possess a unique and remarkable nature
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that these qualities are re-categorized specifically because they are somewhat remarkable.

questions.question: How many qualities of the Lord are identified as being significant enough to warrant a separate, special classification in this context?
questions.explanation: The verse specifically mentions that these eight qualities are being described again in a separate category due to their remarkable nature.

questions.question: What is the primary reason given for the repetition of the eight remarkable qualities within the structure of the text?
options: To provide a complete list of all sixty-four qualities
To contrast them with the qualities of ordinary living entities
To highlight their remarkable status as a separate category
To satisfy the inquiry of a specific disciple
questions.correct_answer: To highlight their remarkable status as a separate category
questions.explanation: The verse notes that because these eight qualities are somewhat remarkable, they have been described again in a separate category.

questions.question: Regarding the eight qualities previously discussed, what status is now assigned to them by virtue of their remarkable nature?
options: They are now considered secondary to the sixty-four primary qualities
They are placed in a separate, distinct category
They are categorized as external symptoms of bhakti
They are merged into the general description of the Lord's form
questions.correct_answer: They are placed in a separate, distinct category
questions.explanation: The text confirms that due to being remarkable, they have been described again in a separate category.

2.1.272.verse_text:
Kṛṣṇa’s assistants:

For matters of dharma, the sages such as Garga; for war, persons such as Sātyaki; and for advice, persons like Uddhava are glorified as assistants to the Lord in revealing these qualities.

questions.question: Regarding the administration of dharma and spiritual guidance, which personality is highlighted as the primary assistant to Lord Kṛṣṇa?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly identifies sages such as Garga as the assistants responsible for matters of dharma.

questions.question: In the context of the Lord's pastimes, which specific personality is recognized for his role as an assistant to Kṛṣṇa in the affairs of war?
questions.explanation: The text mentions persons such as Sātyaki as the assistants glorified for their roles in matters of war.

questions.question: Which personality is celebrated as the esteemed assistant who provides vital advice to the Lord?
questions.explanation: The text specifies persons like Uddhava as the assistants glorified for providing counsel and advice to the Lord.

questions.question: What is the primary function for which sages like Garga are glorified as assistants to Lord Kṛṣṇa?
questions.explanation: The text clearly links sages such as Garga with the responsibility and glory of matters related to dharma.

2.1.273.verse_text:
The devotees of Kṛṣṇa:[177]

Those whose hearts are full of attraction for Kṛṣṇa are called the devotees of Kṛṣṇa.

questions.question: What is the primary internal condition of a person that identifies them as a devotee of Kṛṣṇa?
options: A heart filled with attraction for Kṛṣṇa
The performance of severe penances and austerities
The ability to chant without any offenses
Possessing perfect knowledge of the scriptures
questions.correct_answer: A heart filled with attraction for Kṛṣṇa
questions.explanation: The text explicitly defines the devotee of Kṛṣṇa as one whose heart is full of attraction for Him.

questions.question: How does the text describe the state of the devotee's heart?
options: Burning with the fire of separation
questions.explanation: The scriptural definition provided identifies the heart of the devotee as being saturated with attraction for Kṛṣṇa.

questions.question: According to the definition provided for a Kṛṣṇa-bhakta, what specific quality distinguishes them?
options: Their immense knowledge of the Vedas
Their continuous service to the Lord's feet
The presence of attraction within their heart for the Lord
Their strict adherence to varnashrama duties
questions.correct_answer: The presence of attraction within their heart for the Lord
questions.explanation: The fundamental characteristic defined is the heart's attraction towards Kṛṣṇa.

questions.question: What title is given to those who maintain an internal state of attraction for Kṛṣṇa?
questions.explanation: Those whose hearts are full of attraction for Kṛṣṇa are specifically titled the devotees of Kṛṣṇa.

2.1.274.verse_text: The learned understand that all the qualities of Kṛṣṇa from telling to the truth (2.1.24) to bashfulness (2.1.27) are also present in the devotees of Kṛṣṇa

questions.question: Regarding the glorious qualities of the devotees of Kṛṣṇa, which specific range of divine attributes are said to be present within their hearts?
options: From telling the truth to bashfulness
From sweetness of form to beauty of flute playing
questions.correct_answer: From telling the truth to bashfulness
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that all qualities of Kṛṣṇa starting from truthfulness (the quality mentioned in 2.1.24) up to bashfulness (the quality mentioned in 2.1.27) are present in the devotees.

questions.question: When considering the manifestation of Kṛṣṇa's divine qualities, who is it that acknowledges their presence within the hearts of His devotees?
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that it is the learned who understand that these qualities are present in the devotees.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the relationship between the qualities of Kṛṣṇa and the qualities of His devotees according to the established scriptural conclusion?
options: The devotee possesses unique qualities entirely distinct from Kṛṣṇa
The devotee possesses only a shadow of Kṛṣṇa's qualities
The qualities of Kṛṣṇa are also present in the devotees
The devotee must first earn Kṛṣṇa's qualities through long austerity
questions.correct_answer: The qualities of Kṛṣṇa are also present in the devotees
questions.explanation: The verse clearly confirms that the qualities ranging from truthfulness to bashfulness are found within the devotees of Kṛṣṇa.

questions.question: In the context of the qualities discussed as being present in the devotee, what is the upper limit of the range of attributes identified?
questions.explanation: The text defines the scope of these qualities as extending up to bashfulness.

2.1.275.verse_text: The devotees are said to be of two types: practitioners (sādhakas) and perfected (siddhas).

questions.question: Into how many primary categories are the devotees of the Supreme Lord classified according to the divisions of devotional attainment?
questions.explanation: The devotees are clearly divided into two specific classifications: practitioners and perfected.

questions.question: Which of the following titles correctly identifies one of the two categories of devotees mentioned in the scriptural classification?
questions.explanation: The scriptural classification identifies practitioners, known as sādhakas, as one of the two types of devotees.

questions.question: When categorizing the devotees of the Lord, what is the term used to describe those who have reached the stage of perfection?
questions.explanation: Those devotees who are perfected are referred to as siddhas in the classification of devotees.

2.1.276.verse_text: Practitioners (sādhaka) are those who have developed rati for Kṛṣṇa but have not completely extinguished the anarthas, and who are qualified to see Kṛṣṇa directly.[178]

questions.question: Which specific term describes the category of devotees who have developed rati for the Supreme Lord but still contend with the presence of anarthas?
questions.explanation: The verse defines sādhakas as those who have developed rati for Kṛṣṇa but have not yet fully cleared all anarthas.

questions.question: What is the primary spiritual advancement that a sādhaka has achieved regarding their relationship with Kṛṣṇa?
options: Attainment of a perfected spiritual body
questions.explanation: A sādhaka is defined as a practitioner who has specifically developed rati for Kṛṣṇa despite the presence of remaining anarthas.

questions.question: Despite the presence of anarthas, what unique qualification defines the spiritual status of a sādhaka?
options: They are qualified to see Kṛṣṇa directly
They have performed all Vedic yajnas
They are liberated from the cycle of birth and death
They possess complete knowledge of all scriptures
questions.correct_answer: They are qualified to see Kṛṣṇa directly
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that sādhakas are qualified to see Kṛṣṇa directly, even while they are still working to extinguish anarthas.

questions.question: What is the status of anarthas in the life of a sādhaka according to their devotional definition?
options: They have not been completely extinguished
They are non-existent from the start of practice
They only appear after the stage of rati
questions.correct_answer: They have not been completely extinguished
questions.explanation: The text notes that for a sādhaka, although rati is developed, the anarthas have not yet been completely extinguished.

2.1.277.verse_text:
An example from the Eleventh Canto:

An intermediate or second-class devotee, called madhyama-adhikārī, offers his love to the Supreme Personality of Godhead, is a sincere friend to all the devotees of the Lord, shows mercy to ignorant people who are innocent and disregards those who are envious of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. SB 11.2.46

questions.question: Regarding the behavior of a madhyama-adhikārī toward the Supreme Personality of Godhead, what is their primary offering?
options: A mixture of fear and devotion
questions.explanation: The madhyama-adhikārī is defined as one who offers his love to the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

questions.question: How does a madhyama-adhikārī interact with fellow devotees of the Lord?
options: They remain aloof to maintain their focus
They serve them as their superiors
They are a sincere friend to them
They test them to ensure they are qualified
questions.correct_answer: They are a sincere friend to them
questions.explanation: The scriptural definition explicitly states that a madhyama-adhikārī acts as a sincere friend to all the devotees of the Lord.

questions.question: What is the specific attitude a madhyama-adhikārī maintains toward innocent people who are ignorant of devotional life?
options: They ignore them until they seek out truth
questions.explanation: It is characteristic of the madhyama-adhikārī to show mercy to ignorant people who are innocent.

questions.question: How should a madhyama-adhikārī respond to individuals who harbor envy toward the Supreme Personality of Godhead?
options: They attempt to convert them through debate
They treat them with formal etiquette
questions.explanation: The scriptural definition teaches that a madhyama-adhikārī disregards those who are envious of the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

2.1.278.verse_text:
Another example:

Do not worry, that, after you have been drenched in tears arising from the river of the pastimes of the Lord, you will remain in the flame of suffering in the material world.
